metadata_information.title: State ownership of financial institutions in Europe and Central Asia
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: State ownership of financial institutions in Europe and Central Asia

document_description.abstract: This paper introduces key findings from a new cross-country survey on state-owned financial institutions in Europe and Central Asia. It covers 41 such institutions operating in the region as of end 2016, and considers variables in the areas of mandates, instruments, performance, governance, and monitoring/evaluation. It presents results over the period following the global financial crisis up to 2015, distinguishes between state-owned commercial and development financial institutions, and looks at differences among three geographical sub-regions.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
